title: calamintha alpina subsp. baumgartenii
intro: Calamintha alpina subsp. baumgartenii, also known as mountain basil or alpine calamint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Calamintha alpina subsp. baumgartenii include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Calamintha alpina subsp. baumgartenii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
